data.title: BioCryst appoints Charlie Gayer to succeed Jon Stonehouse as CEO
data.description:
BioCryst Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:BCRX) said that Chief Executive Officer Jon Stonehouse will retire on December 31.

The board has appointed Charlie Gayer, currently the company’s chief commercial officer, as the company’s next president, effective August 1, and next chief executive officer, effective January 1, 2026.

data.title: Vericel GAAP EPS of -$0.01 beats by $0.02, revenue of $63.24M misses by $1.25M
data.description:
• Vericel press release (NASDAQ:VCEL): Q2 GAAP EPS of -$0.01 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $63.24M (+20.1% Y/Y) misses by $1.25M.
  • Total Revenue Growth of 20% to $63.2 Million, with MACI Revenue Growth of 21% to $53.5 Million

  • 2025 Financial Guidance

    • Reaffirmed MACI full-year revenue growth in the low 20% range
    • Updated Burn Care revenue guidance for the second half of 2025 to be in line with recent run rate of approximately $10 million per quarter
    • Reaffirmed full-year profitability guidance of gross margin of 74% and adjusted EBITDA margin of 26%

data.title: AbbVie in charts: Revenues from Humira -58%, Skyrizi +62%, Rinvoq +42% Y/Y
Lyft boosts its presence in Europe through the acquisition of FREENOW
data.description:
Lyft (NASDAQ:LYFT) announced on Thursday that it has completed the $197 million acquisition of European multi-mobility taxi app FREENOW. The Lyft (NASDAQ:LYFT) app now prompts riders to download FREENOW when opened in FREENOW’s 180 cities across nine countries in Europe.

The acquisition gives Lyft (LYFT) an immediate presence in nine European countries and over 150 cities, including major hubs such as London, Berlin, Madrid, Paris, and Rome—markets where FREENOW is a leader. The deal nearly doubles Lyft's (LYFT) total addressable market to over 300 billion personal vehicle trips per year. 

FREENOW’s strength in digital taxi and multi-modal services (including e-scooters and car-sharing) was noted to complement Lyft's (LYFT) rideshare business and help to diversify the company's revenue streams.

"Every conversation I've had with FREENOW drivers and team members has reminded me why we’re joining forces: to bring the best of each company to the other," stated Lyft CEO David Risher. "This isn't just about growing our business. It's about serving and connecting people worldwide," he added.

FREENOW CEO Thomas Zimmermann and his leadership team will report to Jeremy Bird, EVP of Driver Experience at Lyft. The FREENOW country general managers will continue to strategically lead operations in Ireland, the United Kingdom, Germany, Greece, Spain, Italy, Poland, France, and Austria.

The acquisition will not impact Lyft's (LYFT) Q2 financial results.

data.title: SNDL stock jumps as income turns positive for the first time
data.description:
Shares of SNDL (NASDAQ:SNDL) jumped 8.3% premarket on Thursday after the Canadian marijuana player reported second quarter results financials showing revenue growth and its first-ever positive operating income.

The Calgary-headquartered firm posted C$244.8 million in net revenue, up 7.3% from the same period last year. The growth was mainly driven by a 17.4% jump in the combined Cannabis business and a return to growth in the Liquor retail segment.

The company reported positive operating income of C$5M in the quarter, which includes a -C$0.8 million restructuring charge. Excluding this charge, adjusted operating income totaled C$5.8 million.

Net earnings totaled C$2.88M, compared to a loss of C$4.96M in the prior year quarter.

“Achieving positive quarterly operating income and net earnings across both metrics for the first time in the company's history during Q2 2025 marks a pivotal milestone and underscores the effectiveness of our strategic improvement agenda,” SNDL said.

Gross margin in Q2 stood at 27.6%, matching the record set in the first quarter and marking a 2.1 percentage point increase compared to the same period last year.

However, free cash flow in the second quarter of 2025 was negative at $(7.9) million, as strong P&L performance was outweighed by working capital investments to support international growth in the second half of the year, annual incentive and insurance payments, and capital expenditures for upcoming store openings.
